{"id":"project-LICHEN-f3z3","title":"SLIP transport: stats access without synchronization","description":"File: lichen/subsys/lichen/transport/slip_transport.c
Lines: 572-589

Unlike kiss_transport which uses stats_mutex, slip_transport accesses stats without any synchronization:

1. slip_transport_get_stats() (line 572-582) reads ctx->stats
2. slip_transport_reset_stats() (line 584-589) writes ctx->stats  
3. Various callbacks/threads modify stats (lines 244, 294-296, 299, 315, etc.)

The RX thread modifies stats from slip_dispatch_packet() while the main thread may call get_stats() or reset_stats(). This is a data race.

The issue is structural - there is a tx_mutex defined (line 60) but no stats_mutex.

Fix: Add a stats_mutex and protect all stats reads/writes, or document that stats are best-effort.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:53:38.145301Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-12T06:53:38.145341Z"}

new_value: stats_mutex fully implemented and used everywhere (struct, init, all ++ ops, get/reset). Matches kiss_transport. Race fixed. Bead json updated. Commit 57c611c5d
